steps.title: Translate the percent increase to a growth factor
steps.content: An increase of $190\%$ each hour means the new count equals the old count plus $1.90$ times the old count: $$\text{new}=\text{old}+1.90\cdot\text{old}=2.90\cdot\text{old}.$$ The hourly growth factor is $2.9$.

steps.title: Build the exponential model
steps.content: Exponential growth has the form $C=C_0\cdot r^{t}$. With initial value $C_0=40$ and growth factor $r=2.9$: $$C=40(2.9)^{t}.$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $C=40(1.19)^{t}$ — corresponds to a $19\%$ hourly increase. Eliminated.
  • B) $C=40(1.9)^{t}$ — corresponds to a $90\%$ increase per hour (forgets to add $1$). Eliminated.
  • C) $C=40(19)^{t}$ — corresponds to a $1800\%$ increase per hour. Eliminated.
  • D) $C=40(2.9)^{t}$ — matches the $190\%$ hourly increase.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
